column19: Scheme Twists in the Villain Deck
column23: Comments

I was hoping Boom Goes the Dynamite would help me deal with the wounds but only 1 copy total showed up through both games. Covert Operation let me win by taking down Korvac even though I only hit the mastermind once.

First try i got 4 rares in hq by turn 7, after 6 turns of getting nothing done, i called it reset. Second try did it somehow. Punisher was able to thin the deck enough for nightcrawler and Moon Knight to get to 10 attack. Black Cat actually helped a lot. And warriors were there too, i guess.
Solid setup.  Nothing spectacular about it, but nothing horrible about it either.

On paper, we were very excited with our alternative plan to handle the wounds Mephisto was handing out with Angel's and Beta Ray Bill's discard power. It was moderately successful, but we never saw a single Diving Catch card in either game. Black Cat, on the other hand was very abundant. AntDude's unenjoyable strategy was to simply clear Black Cat and the Warriors Three out of the HQ so WaspWoman could power up her deck with Angel and BRB. Nightcrawler's teleport was very friendly in waiting for Korvac Revealed too!

column2: J. Jonah Jameson Hacks Cerebro Servers To...
column23: I've had pretty lucky VD shuffle and during my entire game I have drawn 3 scheme twists and 0 master strikes. Left hand went with Nick Fury/Agent X-13 Shield combo with all the officers from the Angry mob stack, Right hand used a strategy with Strong Guy and Thing. Squirrel girl was used only for switcheroo but really helped a lot to get the right cards at the right time. 4 attack was not a lot to generate for an Angry Mob/MM fight and I've got super lucky with two Terraxes sitting in the city each with 3 bystanders when Right hand had 4 Strength cards in play. Really amazing game, I still do not know what was the unveiled part of the scheme!
Cap helped me get a lot of recruit to get Squirrel Girls rare very early. Switcharoo helped with Strong Guys formation. Those were the downfalls of Mr Grumpy news editor

Went back and forth on the fun, went one side Strong Guy with Strength and the other side random cards. Felt it was better to get JJJ done as soon as I could. Got lucky that I didn’t have the veiled scheme affect me but was hoping to have it done before it was revealed.

Oof. Really wish we had two attempts on this one. I only failed one twist in the first half but always had a full city, which screwed me in the second half. Thing showed up super late so I barely got to use him. Shatterstar did ok but definitely could have been better. Probably should have saved him for a higher attack mastermind, oh well. Gg

column2: Emperor Vulcan Changes the Outcome of WWII
column23: This was though, I am glad I chose Adam Warlock, without his shards I would have never came close to winning. I chose Falcon for another shot at avoiding wounds, but with 4 master strikes in the first 8 turns, it was a failure. Luckily I have enough discard fodder this way...
Getting Cyclops' rare early proved to be critical; I never got a chance to spend the Throne's Favor, but neither did anyone else.
Scheme was fun, but I just couldn’t manage the city, keep the throne’s favor from Vulcan, and hit Vulcan. I tried. Also Wolverine’s wound-control common was hiding most of the game.

Cap ‘41 and Venom made for great partners, as long as Cap kept Venom from eating all the bystanders they rescued. And Venom helped lengthen the game by slinging bystanders out into each capital before a Scheme Twist. Compared to my second attempt, this was a great game!

column2: Zombie Green Goblin & the God-Emperor of Battleworld
column23: First match was a dirty win. Bad luck with the thoughest Villains right at the beginning; won the game by defeating the God-Emperor after the 7th twist; second game was so much better - MVP was Spider-Gwen rescuing many Bystanders and producing great attack values; my second hand was a Spidey Deck, and I easily had 20-30\\\ almost every turn as soon as I had enough Spidey-Cards. I hate Sentry, and Miek didn't fit with my Spideys either, but I really had to win this game ;-)
Got lucky with the Scheme, and KO'd the God Emperor while it was at 13 attack. Sentry is horrible, and is rocketing down my ranking of Heroes.

Holy sh*t this game went almost perfectly, I had done one where the HQ got clogged with money and couldn't deal any damage, but second try was almost perfect, we did logan Left, yellow + tech and I got a couple sad agoraphobia starting up, luckily they comboed into phase out and energy drain for a quick started deck ko, zombie viper helped by coming back 5 times somehow.
Once logan was capped at 16 dpr with 6 cards... I KOed my recruit and bought Sidekick to draw into 19 dmg, recruiting 1 last turn just to KO for 1 dmg. What a thrill ride.
Logan and Kitty pride forever (Miek helped.. ok even sentry was useful xD)

Second game was going better,  but 8 scheme twists in the first 15 cards made me lose. So game 1 got submitted.  The turn after a rare showed up Zombie Green Goblin got it. I could have bought it too that turn. Leader played extra cards so the next turn would have been a loss for me.

column2: Apocalypse Weaves a Web of Lies
column23: This was probably my best game ever against Apocalypse, I got so lucky so many times

I managed to get Hiroim's rare and Widow's Covert Operation into a 11 card hand with 2 mission accomplished and 2 Dangerous Rescue. That's 4/11 cards giving me bystanders every 2 turns. Then Punisher knocking out my 0pt heroes and some villains too. Second hand wasn't doing much other than crowd control. First time I've really had Widow utilize a Bystander Engine.

With elimination on the line, busted out the guns, and Natasha did not disappoint. Was able to get her engine running early, and actually over KOd that deck. I was very lucky, as the last scheme twist was the last villain card, allowing me to hit Apocalypse twice on that final turn. I needed all the time, as his Strike set me back entire entire turns. Game 2 was a loss…
I just didn't see any of the really good Jean Grey/Black Widow bystander cards until way too late so it took way too long to ramp up.  I hit him 3 times but then got the final twist and lost.

column2: Fin Fang Foom Forges the Infinity Gauntlet
column23: Ooof this setup was hard 🥺

We came close, what killed us was the 3rd hit on FFF killed Arkons rare and made me discard Deadpool's rare. If that hadn't happened, we would have won in turn 41. Final turn was the one card that played extra from the VD so we got that extra turn lol 8 Bystanders saved... 22 KO'd
Got Rogue's rare early which helped win the game along with Groot's rare as well.
Always love playing with Angel. Throw in heroes with Smash and he just gets better!!!

Hulk is King is probably the most satisfying card to play in this game, particularly paired with several discard mechanics. Was a very close result between this and my other game which scored more VPs but took longer. Hope I submitted the right one.
At least this was not Belasco with Massive earthquake generator, where was enough KOd heroes to loose that 4 times

column2: Uru Enchanted Iron Man Imprisons Unregistered Superhumans
column23: Both games ended on Turn 20 oddly enough but this one (the first of the two) definitely outshined. Deadpool slung bystanders into the city and once Black Widow rescued them there was no contest. Hercules/Cyclops/Jessica played well on their own and thinned their deck enough to adequately control the city, but Widow punched on the final turn for 44 attack, it just works.
The theme was almost plausible. All the selected heroes seem likely to oppose such a thing. I was also able to combo pretty well here, and I always enjoy calculated risks.
fast game, early rare pickups of cyclops and skadi helped beating iron man before he could really arm up on uru enchanted weapons

column2: The Hydra Super-Adaptoid's Five Families of Crime
column23: We got Thor & Stingray's best cards after defeating Paibok the Power Skrull. Shards helped fill in gaps with hands with little to no attack.

Submitting first game.  MCU side took draw-junk until I grabbed some Surge of Powers and then waited until a Call Lightning showed up, helped a little with keeping the city clear.  Comic side was able to string together Deathlok/Stingray tech synergy for more consistent fighting, Deck Chairs On The Titanic setting up Superpowered Swimsuit nicely and Guns on my Guns having more targets (core set SHIELD Agents count!).  Second game had 8 escapes in 17 turns.

Wow!! This is the kind of Legendary I love to hate. Tough and gritty to the bitter end. My first match I pulled 4 Scheme Twists in a row from 3 different Villain Decks 😭 Lost that game frustratingly fast.. My second go around was not much better but I came out on top in the end thanks to Caps “Perfect Teamwork” Cards 🛡

I actually won the game against the Tactic “Thor’s Hammer” using Thor’s “Call Lighting” card. I guess the Super-Adaptoid could replicate his hammer but not his lighting 🤣
